title: sorghum dochna var. technicum
intro: Sorghum dochna var. technicum, also known as wild sorghum or broomcorn millet, is a plant species belonging to the Sorghum genus. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to have numerous health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Sorghum dochna var. technicum can be prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine as a powder or tincture.
